content.content:
- content: 'Internal notes: when a customer asks whether an overdraft-related fee
    is

    covered by a grace period, fee forgiven rule, or account fee adjustment

    policy, review the customer''s current account and fee state and then read

    the applicable bank policy. Do not infer applicability from the existence

    of a fee-support page alone. Relevant state may include account type,

    account status, fee type, whether the fee has posted, fee posting date,

    and balance recovery timing. These notes are not a substitute for the

    official policy and do not decide whether any particular fee qualifies.

    '

args.query: site:wellsfargo.com deposit account agreement overdraft fee grace period next business day

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Wells Fargo Extra Day Grace Period

  - url: https://www.wellsfargo.com/checking/extra-day-grace-period/

  - source: Wells Fargo

  - domain: wellsfargo.com


  ## Content


  ## How Extra Day Grace Period works


  Extra Day Grace Period is Wells Fargo''s overdraft-fee grace period for eligible
  checking-account overdraft items. The page explains that a customer can avoid overdraft
  fees when the available balance is positive by 11:59 PM Eastern Time on the next
  business day after the overdraft item.


  If the grace period ends and the available balance is not positive, Wells Fargo
  may charge a $35 fee for each overdraft item, subject to the daily maximum that
  applies to consumer checking accounts.


  Examples of deposits or credits that can increase the available balance during the
  same business day include direct deposit, a cash deposit, a transfer from another
  account, a wire transfer, and Zelle. The page also notes that other transactions
  may reduce the available balance during the extra day, including debit-card purchases,
  transfers out of the account, and other payments.


  ## Example


  The page gives an example in which a customer starts Thursday with an available
  balance of $20. That night Wells Fargo pays a check for $80, leaving the account
  overdrawn by $60 and subject to a $35 overdraft fee. On Friday, Wells Fargo receives
  a deposit of $75, and there are no other pending transactions by 11:59 PM Eastern
  Time. In that situation, the overdraft fee from the prior night''s check is waived
  because the available balance is sufficient to cover the overdraft item.


  ## Multiple overdraft items


  When there is more than one prior-business-day overdraft item, Extra Day Grace Period
  applies the available balance as of 11:59 PM Eastern Time to those overdraft items
  in the order they posted. The customer should deposit or transfer enough money to
  cover all of the overdrawn balance. If the available balance covers only some, but
  not all, of the overdraft items, the customer may still incur overdraft fees on
  items that are not covered.


  ## Fee and account-scope notes


  Wells Fargo states that the overdraft fee for Consumer checking accounts is $35
  per item, whether the overdraft is by check, ATM withdrawal, debit card transaction,
  or other electronic means. Wells Fargo charges no more than three overdraft fees
  per business day for Consumer checking accounts.


  Overdraft fees do not apply to Clear Access Banking accounts. Other fees may apply,
  and a Clear Access Banking account can still have a negative balance. The page refers
  customers to the Wells Fargo Consumer Account Fee and Information Schedule and the
  Deposit Account Agreement for details.


  The page also explains that available balance is the bank''s current record of funds
  available for use or withdrawal. It includes posted deposits and withdrawals and
  adjusts for holds on recent deposits and known pending transactions. It may not
  reflect all transactions, such as checks written or debit-card transactions that
  have been approved but not yet submitted for payment by the merchant.


  Payment of transactions into overdraft is discretionary. Wells Fargo says it reserves
  the right not to pay transactions into overdraft, and customers must promptly bring
  an overdrawn account to a positive balance.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Wells Fargo Extra Day Grace Period
url: https://www.wellsfargo.com/checking/extra-day-grace-period/

Based on your account records, Wells Fargo’s **Extra Day Grace Period does not appear to apply** to this overdraft fee.

- **Account:** Everyday Checking — an account type that can be eligible.
- **Overdraft item posted:** July 6, 2026.
- **Grace-period deadline:** 11:59 p.m. Eastern on July 7, 2026.
- **Balance at the deadline:** **–$12.44**, so it was not positive.
- **First positive balance:** July 8 at 10:15 a.m., after the deadline.
- **Fee status:** Posted overdraft fee.

Wells Fargo says the available balance must be positive by **11:59 p.m. Eastern on the next business day** after the overdraft item. Since your balance remained negative at that point, the fee was not waived under the Extra Day rule.

Source: [Wells Fargo Extra Day Grace Period](https://www.wellsfargo.com/checking/extra-day-grace-period/)

You could still contact Wells Fargo and ask whether they will make a courtesy adjustment, but the account timeline does not meet the stated grace-period requirement.

final_answer:
Based on your account records, Wells Fargo’s **Extra Day Grace Period does not appear to apply** to this overdraft fee.

- **Account:** Everyday Checking — an account type that can be eligible.
- **Overdraft item posted:** July 6, 2026.
- **Grace-period deadline:** 11:59 p.m. Eastern on July 7, 2026.
- **Balance at the deadline:** **–$12.44**, so it was not positive.
- **First positive balance:** July 8 at 10:15 a.m., after the deadline.
- **Fee status:** Posted overdraft fee.

Wells Fargo says the available balance must be positive by **11:59 p.m. Eastern on the next business day** after the overdraft item. Since your balance remained negative at that point, the fee was not waived under the Extra Day rule.

Source: [Wells Fargo Extra Day Grace Period](https://www.wellsfargo.com/checking/extra-day-grace-period/)

You could still contact Wells Fargo and ask whether they will make a courtesy adjustment, but the account timeline does not meet the stated grace-period requirement.
